   142→
   143→def _pipelined_multi_all_gather_and_consume(
   144→    shard: list[torch.Tensor],
   145→    shard_consumer: Callable[[list[torch.Tensor], int], None],
   146→    ag_out: list[torch.Tensor],
   147→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   148→    ag_out_needed: bool = True,
   149→) -> None:
   150→    """
   151→    Perform the following logic with micro-pipelined computation and
   152→    communication:
   153→
   154→        gathered = [
   155→            all_gather_tensor(x, gather_dim=0, group=group)
   156→            for x in shard
   157→        ]
   158→
   159→        shards = [[] for _ in range(group_size)]
   160→        for x in ag_out:
   161→            for i, y in enumerate(x.chunk(group_size)):
   162→                shards[i].append(y)
   163→
   164→        for src_rank, shard in enumerate(shards):
   165→            shard_consumer(shard, src_rank)
   166→    """
   167→    p2p_workspace_size_req = 0
   168→    for x in shard:
   169→        p2p_workspace_size_req += x.numel() * x.element_size()
   170→    symm_mem = get_symm_mem_workspace(group_name, min_size=p2p_workspace_size_req)
   171→    group_size = symm_mem.world_size
   172→    rank = symm_mem.rank
   173→
   174→    symm_mem.barrier(channel=0)
   175→    backend_stream = _get_backend_stream()
   176→    backend_stream.wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
   177→
   178→    for x, y in zip(shard, ag_out):
   179→        assert x.is_contiguous(), (
   180→            "_pipelined_all_gather_and_consume: all tensors "
   181→            "in `shard` must be contiguous"
   182→        )
   183→        assert y.is_contiguous(), (
   184→            "_pipelined_all_gather_and_consume: all tensors "
   185→            "in `ag_out` must be contiguous"
   186→        )
   187→        assert x.shape[0] * group_size == y.shape[0]
   188→        assert x.shape[1:] == y.shape[1:]
   189→
   190→    def copy_shard(dst: list[torch.Tensor], src: list[torch.Tensor]) -> None:
   191→        for d, s in zip(dst, src):
   192→            d.copy_(s)
   193→
   194→    def get_p2p_bufs(remote_rank: int) -> list[torch.Tensor]:
   195→        offset_bytes = 0
   196→        bufs = []
   197→        for x in shard:
   198→            buf = symm_mem.get_buffer(
   199→                remote_rank,
   200→                x.shape,
   201→                x.dtype,
   202→                storage_offset=offset_bytes // x.element_size(),
   203→            )
   204→            bufs.append(buf)
   205→            offset_bytes += buf.numel() * buf.element_size()
   206→        return bufs
   207→
   208→    local_p2p_bufs = get_p2p_bufs(rank)
   209→
   210→    # shards[i] => shard from rank i
   211→    shards: list[list[torch.Tensor]] = [[] for _ in range(group_size)]
   212→    for x in ag_out:
   213→        for i, y in enumerate(x.chunk(group_size)):
   214→            shards[i].append(y)
   215→
   216→    # Parallelization strategy: after each rank copies its shard into its local
   217→    # p2p buffer, every rank issues independent p2p copy -> shard_consumer
   218→    # sequences to two streams. In addition to computation/communication
   219→    # overlapping, the strategy allows for computation/computation overlapping,
   220→    # greatly reducing quantization inefficiency.
   221→    #
   222→    # Notation:
   223→    # - "mv" for the copy to local buffer
   224→    # - "cp" for p2p copies
   225→    # - "b" for barriers
   226→    #
   227→    # Constraints:
   228→    # - The GPU scheduler may or may not overlap "mv" with the first shard_consumer.
   229→    # - "cp" from different streams cannot overlap.
   230→    #
   231→    # Ideal scenario 0 - "mv" overlaps with the first shard_consumer:
   232→    #
   233→    # stream 0: [ shard_consumer ][ cp ][ shard_consumer ]
   234→    # stream 1: [ mv ][b][ cp ][ shard_consumer ]
   235→    #
   236→    # Ideal scenario 1 - "mv" is scheduled before the first shard_consumer:
   237→    #
   238→    # stream 0:       [ shard_consumer ][ cp ][ shard_consumer ]
   239→    # stream 1: [ mv ][b][ cp ][ shard_consumer ]
   240→    #
   241→    # Suboptimal scenario 0 - "mv" is scheduled after the first shard_consumer:
   242→    #
   243→    # stream 0: [ shard_consumer ]               [ cp ][ shard_consumer ]
   244→    # stream 1:                   [ mv ][b][ cp ][ shard_consumer ]
   245→    #
   246→    # Suboptimal scenario 0 - "b" is scheduled after the first shard_consumer:
   247→    #
   248→    # stream 0:       [ shard_consumer ]         [ cp ][ shard_consumer ]
   249→    # stream 1: [ mv ]                  [b][ cp ][ shard_consumer ]
   250→    #
   251→    # We haven't yet figured out a way to ensure "mv" and "b" are either
   252→    # overlapped with or scheduled before the first shard_consumer. Thus, to
   253→    # prevent suboptimal scenarios, we are giving up the chance to overlap "mv"
   254→    # and "b" with the first shard_consumer for now.
   255→    copy_shard(dst=local_p2p_bufs, src=shard)
   256→    symm_mem.barrier(channel=1)
   257→    backend_stream.wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
   258→
   259→    # At this point, all ranks have copied their local shard to
   260→    # their local p2p buffer. Each rank can now copy and consume
   261→    # remote shards.
   262→    shard_consumer(shard, rank)
   263→
   264→    for step in range(1, group_size):
   265→        if step % 2 == 0:
   266→            stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
   267→        else:
   268→            stream = backend_stream
   269→        remote_rank = (step + rank) % group_size
   270→        remote_p2p_bufs = get_p2p_bufs(remote_rank)
   271→        with stream:
   272→            copy_shard(dst=shards[remote_rank], src=remote_p2p_bufs)
   273→            shard_consumer(shards[remote_rank], remote_rank)
   274→
   275→    if ag_out_needed:
   276→        # Copy from input to the all-gather output. Opportunistically overlap
   277→        # it with the last shard_consumer.
   278→        if group_size % 2 == 0:
   279→            stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
   280→        else:
   281→            stream = backend_stream
   282→        with stream:
   283→            copy_shard(dst=shards[rank], src=shard)
   284→
   285→    torch.cuda.current_stream().wait_stream(backend_stream)
   286→    symm_mem.barrier(channel=0)
   287→
   288→
   289→def _pipelined_all_gather_and_consume(
   290→    shard: torch.Tensor,
   291→    shard_consumer: Callable[[torch.Tensor, int], None],
   292→    ag_out: torch.Tensor,
   293→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   294→    ag_out_needed: bool = True,
   295→) -> None:
   296→    """
   297→    Perform the following logic with micro-pipelined computation and
   298→    communication:
   299→
   300→        ag_out = all_gather_tensor(shard, gather_dim=0, group=group)
   301→        shards = ag_out.chunk(group.size())
   302→        for src_rank, shard in enumerate(shards):
   303→            shard_consumer(shard, src_rank)
   304→    """
   305→
   306→    def adapter(shard: list[torch.Tensor], rank: int) -> None:
   307→        shard_consumer(shard[0], rank)
   308→
   309→    _pipelined_multi_all_gather_and_consume(
   310→        [shard],
   311→        adapter,
   312→        [ag_out],
   313→        group_name,
   314→        ag_out_needed,
   315→    )
   316→
   317→
   318→def _pipelined_produce_and_all2all(
   319→    chunk_producer: Callable[[int, torch.Tensor], None],
   320→    output: torch.Tensor,
   321→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   322→    out_chunk_dim: int = 0,
   323→) -> None:
   324→    """
   325→    Perform the following logic with micro-pipelined computation and
   326→    communication:
   327→
   328→        chunks = [
   329→            chunk_producer(dst_rank, chunks[dst_rank])
   330→            for dst_rank in range(group_size):
   331→        ]
   332→        dist.all_to_all_single(output=output, input=torch.cat(chunks))
   333→    """
   334→    out_chunks = output.chunk(
   335→        c10d._get_group_size_by_name(group_name), dim=out_chunk_dim
   336→    )
   337→    p2p_workspace_size_req = out_chunks[0].numel() * out_chunks[0].element_size() * 2
   338→    symm_mem = get_symm_mem_workspace(group_name, min_size=p2p_workspace_size_req)
   339→    group_size = symm_mem.world_size
   340→    rank = symm_mem.rank
   341→
   342→    symm_mem.barrier(channel=0)
   343→    backend_stream = _get_backend_stream()
   344→    backend_stream.wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
   345→
   346→    def get_p2p_buf(rank: int, idx: int) -> torch.Tensor:
   347→        assert idx in (0, 1)
   348→        offset = 0 if idx == 0 else out_chunks[0].numel()
   349→        return symm_mem.get_buffer(
   350→            rank, out_chunks[0].shape, out_chunks[0].dtype, offset
   351→        )
   352→
   353→    # Prepare two local p2p buffers, so that a remote rank can pull the result
   354→    # of step [i] in one p2p buffer while the local rank can compute the
   355→    # result of step [i+1] and write it directly the other p2p buffer.
   356→    local_p2p_buf_0 = get_p2p_buf(rank, 0)
   357→    local_p2p_buf_1 = get_p2p_buf(rank, 1)
   358→
   359→    for step in range(1, group_size):
   360→        remote_rank = (rank - step) % group_size
   361→        if step % 2 == 0:
   362→            stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
   363→            p2p_buf = local_p2p_buf_1
   364→            remote_p2p_buf = get_p2p_buf(remote_rank, 1)
   365→        else:
   366→            stream = backend_stream
   367→            p2p_buf = local_p2p_buf_0
   368→            remote_p2p_buf = get_p2p_buf(remote_rank, 0)
   369→        with stream:
   370→            # Parallelization strategy: every rank issues independent compute
   371→            # -> barrier -> p2p copy sequences on two streams. In addition to
   372→            # computation/communication overlapping, the strategy allows for
   373→            # computation/computation overlapping, greatly reducing
   374→            # quantization inefficiency.
   375→            #
   376→            # Ideally, stream activities would look like this ("b" for
   377→            # barriers, "cp" for p2p copies):
   378→            #
   379→            # [rank 0]
   380→            # stream 0:         [  chunk_producer  ][b][ cp ][  chunk_producer ][b][ cp ]
   381→            # stream 1: [  chunk_producer  ][b][ cp ][  chunk_producer  ][b][ cp ]
   382→            #
   383→            # [rank 1]
   384→            # stream 0:         [  chunk_producer  ][b][ cp ][  chunk_producer ][b][ cp ]
   385→            # stream 1: [  chunk_producer  ][b][ cp ][  chunk_producer  ][b][ cp ]
   386→            #
   387→            # Note that the barriers synchronize streams with the same ID
   388→            # across ranks. They don't synchronize streams on the same rank.
   389→            #
   390→            # Since the work on both streams is independent, there's no
   391→            # guarantee that the chunk_producer from stream 0 or stream 1 will
   392→            # be scheduled first. If there is a scheduling mismatch across
   393→            # ranks, the barrier forces all ranks to wait for the slowest.
   394→            #
   395→            # When scheduling mismatches occur among ranks, the stream
   396→            # activities might look like this (note that p2p copies from
   397→            # different streams cannot overlap with each other):
   398→            #
   399→            # [rank 0]
   400→            # stream 0: [  chunk_producer  ][b        ][ cp ][  chunk_producer ][b       ][ cp ]
   401→            # stream 1:         [  chunk_producer  ][b]      [ cp ][  chunk_producer  ][b]      [ cp ]
   402→            #
   403→            # [rank 1]
   404→            # stream 0:         [  chunk_producer  ][b]      [ cp ][  chunk_producer  ][b]      [ cp ]
   405→            # stream 1: [  chunk_producer  ][b        ][ cp ][  chunk_producer  ][b      ][ cp ]
   406→            #
   407→            # To prevent this, we need to ensure that the chunk_producer on
   408→            # stream 1 gets scheduled first on every rank. Without access to
   409→            # the underlying kernels, CUDA offers no API to control the
   410→            # scheduling order of two independent, overlapping kernels. Our
   411→            # solution is to issue a small sleep kernel in stream 0. The sleep
   412→            # duration is insignificant, but having an extra task in stream 0
   413→            # will almost guarantee that the chunk_producer on stream 1 gets
   414→            # scheduled first. Once the first chunk_producer is scheduled in
   415→            # the correct order, there's very little room for the scheduling
   416→            # order of subsequent kernels to be inconsistent across ranks.
   417→            if step == 2:
   418→                torch.cuda._sleep(100)
   419→            chunk_producer((rank + step) % group_size, p2p_buf)
   420→            symm_mem.barrier(channel=step % 2)
   421→            out_chunks[remote_rank].copy_(remote_p2p_buf)
   422→            # The local P2P buffer can only be overwritten by the next
   423→            # chunk_producer after all peers have finished reading from it.
   424→            symm_mem.barrier(channel=step % 2)
   425→
   426→    # If the sleep wasn't issued in the above loop, do it now.
   427→    if group_size == 2:
   428→        torch.cuda._sleep(100)
   429→
   430→    chunk_producer(rank, out_chunks[rank])
   431→    torch.cuda.current_stream().wait_stream(backend_stream)
   432→    symm_mem.barrier(channel=0)
   433→
   434→
   435→lib = torch.library.Library("symm_mem", "DEF")  # noqa: TOR901
   436→lib.define(
   437→    "fused_all_gather_matmul("
   438→    "Tensor A, Tensor[] Bs, int gather_dim, str group_name, *, bool return_A = True) -> (Tensor?, Tensor[])",
   439→    tags=[torch._C.Tag.needs_fixed_stride_order],
   440→)
   441→lib.define(
   442→    "fused_all_gather_scaled_matmul("
   443→    "Tensor A, Tensor[] Bs, Tensor A_scale, Tensor[] B_scales, "
   444→    "int gather_dim, str group_name, "
   445→    "Tensor?[] biases, "
   446→    "Tensor?[] result_scales, "
   447→    "ScalarType?[] out_dtypes, "
   448→    "bool[] use_fast_accum) -> (Tensor, Tensor[])",
   449→    tags=[torch._C.Tag.needs_fixed_stride_order],
   450→)
   451→lib.define(
   452→    "fused_matmul_reduce_scatter(Tensor A, Tensor B, str reduce_op, int scatter_dim, str group_name) -> Tensor",
   453→    tags=[torch._C.Tag.needs_fixed_stride_order],
   454→)
   455→lib.define(
   456→    "fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter("
   457→    "Tensor A, Tensor B, Tensor A_scale, Tensor B_scale, "
   458→    "str reduce_op, int orig_scatter_dim, int scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape, str group_name, SymInt[]? output_shape, "
   459→    "Tensor? bias = None, "
   460→    "Tensor? result_scale = None, "
   461→    "ScalarType? out_dtype = None, "
   462→    "bool use_fast_accum = False) -> Tensor",
   463→    tags=[torch._C.Tag.needs_fixed_stride_order],
   464→)
   465→lib.define("_low_contention_all_gather(Tensor tensor, str group_name) -> Tensor")
   466→lib.define(
   467→    "_low_contention_reduce_scatter(Tensor tensor, str reduce_op, str group_name) -> Tensor"
   468→)
   469→
   470→lib.define("get_remote_tensors(Tensor x, str group_name) -> Tensor[]")
   471→"""
   472→Given a local tensor and a group name, return a tuple of tensors that are
   473→symmetric on other devices. The returned tensors are ordered by rank IDs. The
   474→length of the tuple equals to the size of the group.
   475→
   476→Note: this API works only when `world_within_direct_access()` returns True, i.e.
   477→only when the group is within NVLink domain or similar. It does not work across
   478→network interfaces.
   479→"""
   480→
   481→
   482→@torch.library.impl(lib, "get_remote_tensors", "CUDA")
   483→def _get_remote_tensors_default(
   484→    local: torch.Tensor, group_name: c10d.GroupName
   485→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, ...]:
   486→    hdl = rendezvous(local, group_name)
   487→    if hdl is None:
   488→        raise ValueError("Tensor is not allocated from Symmetric Memory")
   489→
   490→    return tuple(
   491→        hdl.get_remote_tensor(peer, local.size(), local.dtype)
   492→        for peer in range(hdl.world_size)
   493→    )
   494→
   495→
   496→@torch.library.impl(lib, "get_remote_tensors", "Meta")
   497→def _get_remote_tensors_meta(
   498→    local: torch.Tensor, group_name: c10d.GroupName
   499→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, ...]:
   500→    group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
   501→    return tuple(torch.empty_like(local) for _ in range(group.size()))
   502→
   503→
   504→class _ScaleMode(Enum):
   505→    UNSCALED = "unscaled"
   506→    TENSOR_WISE = "tensor-wise"
   507→    ROW_WISE_SHARDED = "row-wise-sharded"
   508→    ROW_WISE_REPLICATED = "row-wise-replicated"
   509→
   510→
   511→def _check_and_verify_fp8_all_gather_scale_mode(
   512→    shard: torch.Tensor, scale: torch.Tensor | None, gather_dim: int, group_size: int
   513→) -> _ScaleMode:
   514→    full_shape = list(shard.shape)
   515→    full_shape[gather_dim] *= group_size
   516→
   517→    if scale is None:
   518→        return _ScaleMode.UNSCALED
   519→    elif scale.shape[:-1] == shard.shape[:-1] and scale.shape[-1] == 1:
   520→        # Row-wise scaling
   521→        #
   522→        # NOTE: when the last dim of both A_shard and A_scale is one, we can't
   523→        # tell if A_scale is replicated tensor-wise scale or sharded row-wise
   524→        # scale. Treating it as row-wise scaling for safety.
   525→        return _ScaleMode.ROW_WISE_SHARDED
   526→    elif scale.numel() == 1:
   527→        return _ScaleMode.TENSOR_WISE
   528→    elif list(scale.shape[:-1]) == full_shape[:-1]:
   529→        return _ScaleMode.ROW_WISE_REPLICATED
   530→    else:
   531→        raise ValueError(
   532→            "Invalid scale shape for fp8 all-gather "
   533→            f"(shard shape: {shard.shape}, scale shape: {scale.shape})"
   534→        )
   535→
   536→
   537→def _fused_all_gather_matmul_impl(
   538→    mm_out_op: torch._ops.OpOverload,
   539→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
   540→    Bs: list[torch.Tensor],
   541→    A_scale: torch.Tensor | None,
   542→    kwargs_list: list[dict[str, Any]],
   543→    out_dtypes: list[torch.dtype | None],
   544→    gather_dim: int,
   545→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   546→    return_A: bool,
   547→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor | None, list[torch.Tensor]]:
   548→    if A_shard.dim() < 2:
   549→        raise ValueError("A_shard must be a matrix")
   550→    for B in Bs:
   551→        if B.dim() != 2:
   552→            raise ValueError("B must be a matrix")
   553→    if len(out_dtypes) != len(Bs):
   554→        raise ValueError("len(out_types) must be the same as len(Bs)")
   555→    if len(kwargs_list) != len(Bs):
   556→        raise ValueError("len(kwargs_list) must be the same as len(Bs)")
   557→    if gather_dim < 0 or gather_dim >= A_shard.dim():
   558→        raise ValueError("Invalid gather_dim")
   559→
   560→    group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
   561→
   562→    if gather_dim == A_shard.ndim - 1 or gather_dim == -1:
   563→        return _fused_all_gather_matmul_last_gather_dim_impl(
   564→            mm_out_op,
   565→            A_shard,
   566→            Bs,
   567→            A_scale,
   568→            kwargs_list,
   569→            out_dtypes,
   570→            gather_dim,
   571→            group_name,
   572→            return_A,
   573→        )
   574→
   575→    # Move the gather_dim to the front and flatten the tensor into a 2D matrix.
   576→    # The flattened tensor doesn't need to be contiguous (for computation
   577→    # efficiency), as _pipelined_all_gather_and_consume guarantees that shards
   578→    # passed to shard_consumer are contiguous.
   579→    A_shard_flat = A_shard.movedim(gather_dim, 0)
   580→    leading_dims = [group.size()] + list(A_shard_flat.shape[:-1])
   581→    A_shard_flat = A_shard_flat.flatten(0, -2)
   582→
   583→    # Helper function for reverting the above transformation
   584→    def unflatten(t: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   585→        return t.view(*leading_dims, -1).flatten(0, 1).movedim(0, gather_dim)
   586→
   587→    A_flat = A_shard_flat.new_empty(
   588→        A_shard_flat.shape[0] * group.size(),
   589→        A_shard_flat.shape[1],
   590→    )
   591→
   592→    outputs = [
   593→        A_flat.new_empty(A_flat.shape[0], B.shape[1], dtype=out_dtype or B.dtype)
   594→        for B, out_dtype in zip(Bs, out_dtypes)
   595→    ]
   596→    output_shards = [output.chunk(group.size()) for output in outputs]
   597→
   598→    scale_mode = _check_and_verify_fp8_all_gather_scale_mode(
   599→        shard=A_shard, scale=A_scale, gather_dim=gather_dim, group_size=group.size()
   600→    )
   601→
   602→    # Computing block-wise matmul along the first dim of A
   603→    if scale_mode == _ScaleMode.ROW_WISE_SHARDED:
   604→        assert A_scale is not None
   605→        A_scale_shard = A_scale.movedim(gather_dim, 0).flatten(0, -2)
   606→        A_scale_flat = A_scale_shard.new_empty(
   607→            A_scale_shard.shape[0] * group.size(),
   608→            A_scale_shard.shape[1],
   609→        )
   610→
   611→        def row_wise_sharded_consumer(shard: list[torch.Tensor], rank: int) -> None:
   612→            for idx, (B, kwargs) in enumerate(zip(Bs, kwargs_list)):
   613→                mm_out_op(
   614→                    shard[0],
   615→                    B,
   616→                    scale_a=shard[1],
   617→                    **kwargs,
   618→                    out=output_shards[idx][rank],
   619→                )
   620→
   621→        _pipelined_multi_all_gather_and_consume(
   622→            [A_shard_flat, A_scale_shard],
   623→            row_wise_sharded_consumer,
   624→            [A_flat, A_scale_flat],
   625→            group_name,
   626→            return_A,
   627→        )
   628→    elif scale_mode == _ScaleMode.ROW_WISE_REPLICATED:
   629→        assert A_scale is not None
   630→        A_scale_shards = (
   631→            A_scale.movedim(gather_dim, 0).flatten(0, -2).chunk(group.size())
   632→        )
   633→
   634→        def row_wise_replicated_consumer(shard: torch.Tensor, rank: int) -> None:
   635→            for idx, (B, kwargs) in enumerate(zip(Bs, kwargs_list)):
   636→                mm_out_op(
   637→                    shard,
   638→                    B,
   639→                    scale_a=A_scale_shards[rank],
   640→                    **kwargs,
   641→                    out=output_shards[idx][rank],
   642→                )
   643→
   644→        _pipelined_all_gather_and_consume(
   645→            A_shard_flat,
   646→            row_wise_replicated_consumer,
   647→            A_flat,
   648→            group_name,
   649→            return_A,
   650→        )
   651→    else:
   652→        if scale_mode == _ScaleMode.TENSOR_WISE:
   653→            assert A_scale is not None
   654→            for kwargs in kwargs_list:
   655→                kwargs["scale_a"] = A_scale
   656→        else:
   657→            assert scale_mode == _ScaleMode.UNSCALED
   658→
   659→        def default_consumer(shard: torch.Tensor, rank: int) -> None:
   660→            for idx, (B, kwargs) in enumerate(zip(Bs, kwargs_list)):
   661→                mm_out_op(shard, B, **kwargs, out=output_shards[idx][rank])
   662→
   663→        _pipelined_all_gather_and_consume(
   664→            A_shard_flat,
   665→            default_consumer,
   666→            A_flat,
   667→            group_name,
   668→            return_A,
   669→        )
   670→
   671→    A = unflatten(A_flat) if return_A else None
   672→    return A, [unflatten(output) for output in outputs]
   673→
   674→
   675→def _pipelined_all_gather_and_consume_last_dim(
   676→    shard: torch.Tensor,
   677→    shard_consumer: Callable[[torch.Tensor, int], None],
   678→    ag_out: torch.Tensor,
   679→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   680→    ag_out_needed: bool = True,
   681→) -> None:
   682→    p2p_workspace_size_req = 0
   683→    p2p_workspace_size_req = shard.numel() * shard.element_size()
   684→    symm_mem = get_symm_mem_workspace(group_name, min_size=p2p_workspace_size_req)
   685→    group_size = symm_mem.world_size
   686→    rank = symm_mem.rank
   687→
   688→    symm_mem.barrier(channel=0)
   689→    backend_stream = _get_backend_stream()
   690→    backend_stream.wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
   691→
   692→    def copy_shard(dst: torch.Tensor, src: torch.Tensor) -> None:
   693→        dst.copy_(src)
   694→
   695→    def get_p2p_buf(remote_rank: int) -> torch.Tensor:
   696→        buf = symm_mem.get_buffer(
   697→            remote_rank,
   698→            shard.shape,
   699→            shard.dtype,
   700→        )
   701→        return buf
   702→
   703→    local_p2p_buf = get_p2p_buf(rank)
   704→
   705→    shards = ag_out.chunk(group_size)
   706→
   707→    copy_shard(dst=local_p2p_buf, src=shard)
   708→    symm_mem.barrier(channel=1)
   709→    backend_stream.wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
   710→
   711→    # At this point, all ranks have copied their local shard to
   712→    # their local p2p buffer. Each rank can now copy and consume
   713→    # remote shards.
   714→    shard_consumer(shard, rank)
   715→
   716→    for step in range(1, group_size):
   717→        if step % 2 == 0:
   718→            stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
   719→        else:
   720→            stream = backend_stream
   721→        remote_rank = (step + rank) % group_size
   722→        remote_p2p_buf = get_p2p_buf(remote_rank)
   723→        with stream:
   724→            copy_shard(dst=shards[remote_rank], src=remote_p2p_buf)
   725→            shard_consumer(shards[remote_rank], remote_rank)
   726→
   727→    if ag_out_needed:
   728→        # Copy from input to the all-gather output. Opportunistically overlap
   729→        # it with the last shard_consumer.
   730→        if group_size % 2 == 0:
   731→            stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
   732→        else:
   733→            stream = backend_stream
   734→        with stream:
   735→            copy_shard(dst=shards[rank], src=shard)
   736→
   737→    torch.cuda.current_stream().wait_stream(backend_stream)
   738→    symm_mem.barrier(channel=0)
   739→
   740→
   741→def _fused_all_gather_matmul_last_gather_dim_impl(
   742→    mm_out_op: torch._ops.OpOverload,
   743→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
   744→    Bs: list[torch.Tensor],
   745→    A_scale: torch.Tensor | None,
   746→    kwargs_list: list[dict[str, Any]],
   747→    out_dtypes: list[torch.dtype | None],
   748→    gather_dim: int,
   749→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   750→    return_A: bool,
   751→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor | None, list[torch.Tensor]]:
   752→    group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
   753→    group_size = group.size()
   754→
   755→    B_shards = [B.chunk(group.size()) for B in Bs]
   756→
   757→    leading_dims = list(A_shard.shape[:-1])
   758→    A_shard_flat = A_shard.flatten(0, -2)
   759→
   760→    def unflatten(t: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
   761→        return t.view(*leading_dims, -1)
   762→
   763→    A_flat_out = A_shard_flat.new_empty(
   764→        A_shard_flat.shape[0] * group.size(),
   765→        A_shard_flat.shape[1],
   766→    )
   767→
   768→    outputs = [
   769→        torch.empty(
   770→            (A_shard_flat.shape[0], B.shape[1]),
   771→            dtype=out_dtype or B.dtype,
   772→            device=A_shard.device,
   773→        )
   774→        for B, out_dtype in zip(Bs, out_dtypes)
   775→    ]
   776→
   777→    first = True
   778→    events = [torch.cuda.Event() for _ in outputs]
   779→
   780→    def default_consumer(shard: torch.Tensor, rank: int) -> None:
   781→        nonlocal first
   782→        for out, event, B_shard, kwargs in zip(outputs, events, B_shards, kwargs_list):
   783→            event.wait()
   784→            if first:
   785→                torch.ops.aten.mm.out(shard, B_shard[rank], **kwargs, out=out)
   786→            else:
   787→                out.addmm_(shard, B_shard[rank])
   788→            event.record()
   789→
   790→        first = False
   791→
   792→    _pipelined_all_gather_and_consume_last_dim(
   793→        A_shard_flat,
   794→        default_consumer,
   795→        A_flat_out,
   796→        group_name,
   797→        return_A,
   798→    )
   799→    ret_A = None
   800→    if return_A:
   801→        # This path is inefficient and will be filtered out at passes stage
   802→        # Added only for completeness.
   803→        A_split_cat_out_flat = torch.cat(A_flat_out.chunk(group_size), dim=-1)
   804→        ret_A = unflatten(A_split_cat_out_flat)
   805→
   806→    return ret_A, [unflatten(output) for output in outputs]
   807→
   808→
   809→@torch.library.impl(lib, "fused_all_gather_matmul", "Meta")
   810→def _fused_all_gather_matmul_fallback(
   811→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
   812→    Bs: list[torch.Tensor],
   813→    gather_dim: int,
   814→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   815→    *,
   816→    return_A: bool = True,
   817→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor | None, list[torch.Tensor]]:
   818→    group_size = c10d._get_group_size_by_name(group_name)
   819→    A = torch.ops._c10d_functional.all_gather_into_tensor(
   820→        A_shard.contiguous(), group_size, group_name
   821→    )
   822→    A = torch.ops._c10d_functional.wait_tensor(A)
   823→    if gather_dim == A.ndim - 1 or gather_dim == -1:
   824→        A_splits = A.chunk(group_size)
   825→        A_mm = torch.cat(A_splits, dim=-1)
   826→        res = [torch.matmul(A_mm, B) for B in Bs]
   827→        if return_A:
   828→            return A_mm, res
   829→        else:
   830→            return None, res
   831→
   832→    A = A.view(group_size, *A_shard.shape).movedim(gather_dim + 1, 1).flatten(0, 1)
   833→    res = [torch.matmul(A, B).movedim(0, gather_dim) for B in Bs]
   834→    if return_A:
   835→        return A.movedim(0, gather_dim), res
   836→    else:
   837→        return None, res
   838→
   839→
   840→@torch.library.impl(lib, "fused_all_gather_matmul", "CUDA")
   841→def _fused_all_gather_matmul(
   842→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
   843→    Bs: list[torch.Tensor],
   844→    gather_dim: int,
   845→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   846→    *,
   847→    return_A: bool = True,
   848→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor | None, list[torch.Tensor]]:
   849→    """
   850→    Perform the following logic with micro-pipelined computation and
   851→    communication:
   852→
   853→        all_gather_tensor(A_shard, gather_dim, group_name) @ B
   854→
   855→    Optimal stride order for A_shard - if A_shard.movedim(gather_dim, 0) is
   856→    contiguous, no extra copy is required for input layout transformation.
   857→    Otherwise A_shard needs to be copied once.
   858→    """
   859→    if _is_test_mode:
   860→        return _fused_all_gather_matmul_fallback(
   861→            A_shard, Bs, gather_dim, group_name, return_A=return_A
   862→        )
   863→
   864→    if _should_use_fused_all_gather_matmul_native(A_shard, Bs, gather_dim, group_name):
   865→        group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
   866→        leading_dims = list(A_shard.shape[:-1])
   867→        leading_dims[0] *= group.size()
   868→        A, out = _fused_all_gather_matmul_native(
   869→            A_shard.flatten(0, -2), Bs[0], group_name
   870→        )
   871→        return A.view(*leading_dims, -1), [out.view(*leading_dims, -1)]
   872→
   873→    if _should_use_multimem_all_gather_matmul(
   874→        A_shard, gather_dim, group_name, return_A
   875→    ):
   876→        return None, _multimem_all_gather_matmul(A_shard, Bs, group_name)
   877→
   878→    with torch.profiler.record_function("fused_all_gather_matmul"):
   879→        return _fused_all_gather_matmul_impl(
   880→            torch.ops.aten.mm.out,
   881→            A_shard,
   882→            Bs,
   883→            None,
   884→            [{} for B in Bs],
   885→            [B.dtype for B in Bs],
   886→            gather_dim,
   887→            group_name,
   888→            return_A,
   889→        )
   890→
   891→
   892→def _should_use_fused_all_gather_matmul_native(
   893→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
   894→    Bs: list[torch.Tensor],
   895→    gather_dim: int,
   896→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   897→) -> bool:
   898→    group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
   899→    local_M = math.prod(A_shard.shape[:-1])
   900→
   901→    return (
   902→        "TORCH_SYMM_MEM_ENABLE_NATIVE_ASYNC_TP" in os.environ
   903→        and A_shard.is_contiguous()
   904→        and gather_dim == 0
   905→        # _async_input_mm requires local_M to be divisible by world_size.
   906→        and local_M % group.size() == 0
   907→        # _async_input_mm outperforms the decomposition-based approach when the
   908→        # global M is small.
   909→        and 2048 < local_M * group.size() <= 4096
   910→        # _async_input_mm only supports a single B.
   911→        and len(Bs) == 1
   912→    )
   913→
   914→
   915→def _fused_all_gather_matmul_native(
   916→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
   917→    B: torch.Tensor,
   918→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   919→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
   920→    symm_mem = rendezvous(A_shard, group_name)
   921→    if symm_mem is None:
   922→        symm_mem = get_symm_mem_workspace(
   923→            group_name, A_shard.numel() * A_shard.element_size()
   924→        )
   925→        symm_mem.barrier()
   926→        buf = symm_mem.get_buffer(symm_mem.rank, A_shard.shape, A_shard.dtype)
   927→        buf.copy_(A_shard)
   928→        A_shard = buf
   929→
   930→    rank = symm_mem.rank
   931→    world_size = symm_mem.world_size
   932→
   933→    current_stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
   934→    backend_stream = _get_backend_stream(priority=-1)
   935→
   936→    symm_mem.barrier()
   937→    backend_stream.wait_stream(current_stream)
   938→    current_stream.wait_stream(backend_stream)
   939→
   940→    A = A_shard.new_empty(A_shard.shape[0] * world_size, A_shard.shape[1])
   941→    A_signals = torch.zeros(world_size, dtype=torch.uint32, device=A_shard.device)
   942→    A_shards = A.chunk(world_size)
   943→
   944→    A_shards[rank].copy_(A_shard)
   945→    if not torch.cuda.is_current_stream_capturing():
   946→        _SymmetricMemory.stream_write_value32(A_signals, rank, 1)
   947→    else:
   948→        _SymmetricMemory.memset32(A_signals, offset=rank, val=1, count=1)
   949→
   950→    out = torch.ops.symm_mem._async_input_mm(A, B, A_signals, rank)
   951→    for step in range(1, world_size):
   952→        src_rank = (rank + step) % world_size
   953→        src_buf = symm_mem.get_buffer(src_rank, A_shard.shape, A_shard.dtype)
   954→        with backend_stream:
   955→            A_shards[src_rank].copy_(src_buf)
   956→            if not torch.cuda.is_current_stream_capturing():
   957→                # cuStreamWriteValue32 issues a system level fence before the write
   958→                _SymmetricMemory.stream_write_value32(A_signals, src_rank, 1)
   959→            else:
   960→                _SymmetricMemory.memset32(A_signals, offset=src_rank, val=1, count=1)
   961→
   962→    current_stream.wait_stream(backend_stream)
   963→    backend_stream.wait_stream(current_stream)
   964→
   965→    symm_mem.barrier()
   966→    return A, out
   967→
   968→
   969→def _should_use_multimem_all_gather_matmul(
   970→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
   971→    gather_dim: int,
   972→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   973→    return_A: bool,
   974→) -> bool:
   975→    group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
   976→    local_M = math.prod(A_shard.shape[:-1])
   977→    has_multicast_support = (
   978→        A_shard.device.type == "cuda"
   979→        and _SymmetricMemory.has_multicast_support(
   980→            DeviceType.CUDA, A_shard.device.index
   981→        )
   982→    )
   983→
   984→    return (
   985→        has_multicast_support
   986→        and not return_A
   987→        and A_shard.is_contiguous()
   988→        and gather_dim == 0
   989→        # The heuristic is empirical. We could refine it with a more
   990→        # sophisticated perf model.
   991→        and local_M * group.size() <= 2048
   992→    )
   993→
   994→
   995→def _multimem_all_gather_matmul(
   996→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
   997→    Bs: list[torch.Tensor],
   998→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
   999→) -> list[torch.Tensor]:
  1000→    group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
  1001→    A_shape = torch.Size((A_shard.shape[0] * group.size(), *A_shard.shape[1:]))
  1002→    symm_mem = get_symm_mem_workspace(
  1003→        group_name, A_shape.numel() * A_shard.element_size()
  1004→    )
  1005→    A = symm_mem.get_buffer(symm_mem.rank, A_shape, A_shard.dtype)
  1006→    torch.ops.symm_mem.multimem_all_gather_out(A_shard, group_name, A)
  1007→    return [torch.matmul(A, B) for B in Bs]
  1008→
  1009→
  1010→@torch.library.impl(lib, "fused_all_gather_scaled_matmul", "Meta")
  1011→def _fused_all_gather_scaled_matmul_fallback(
  1012→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
  1013→    Bs: list[torch.Tensor],
  1014→    A_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1015→    B_scales: list[torch.Tensor],
  1016→    gather_dim: int,
  1017→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1018→    biases: list[torch.Tensor | None],
  1019→    result_scales: list[torch.Tensor | None],
  1020→    out_dtypes: list[torch.dtype | None],
  1021→    use_fast_accum: list[bool],
  1022→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, list[torch.Tensor]]:
  1023→    out_dtypes = _maybe_convert_scalar_types_to_dtypes(out_dtypes)
  1024→
  1025→    group_size = c10d._get_group_size_by_name(group_name)
  1026→    A = torch.ops._c10d_functional.all_gather_into_tensor(
  1027→        A_shard.contiguous(), group_size, group_name
  1028→    )
  1029→    A = torch.ops._c10d_functional.wait_tensor(A)
  1030→    A = A.view(group_size, *A_shard.shape).movedim(gather_dim + 1, 1).flatten(0, 1)
  1031→
  1032→    scale_mode = _check_and_verify_fp8_all_gather_scale_mode(
  1033→        shard=A_shard, scale=A_scale, gather_dim=gather_dim, group_size=group_size
  1034→    )
  1035→    if scale_mode == _ScaleMode.ROW_WISE_SHARDED:
  1036→        A_scale_shard = A_scale
  1037→        A_scale = torch.ops._c10d_functional.all_gather_into_tensor(
  1038→            A_scale.contiguous(), group_size, group_name
  1039→        )
  1040→        A_scale = torch.ops._c10d_functional.wait_tensor(A_scale)
  1041→        A_scale = (
  1042→            A_scale.view(group_size, *A_scale_shard.shape)
  1043→            .movedim(gather_dim + 1, 1)
  1044→            .flatten(0, -2)
  1045→        )
  1046→    elif scale_mode == _ScaleMode.ROW_WISE_REPLICATED:
  1047→        A_scale = A_scale.movedim(gather_dim, 0).flatten(0, -2)
  1048→    else:
  1049→        assert scale_mode == _ScaleMode.TENSOR_WISE
  1050→
  1051→    def scaled_matmul(
  1052→        A: torch.Tensor,
  1053→        B: torch.Tensor,
  1054→        A_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1055→        B_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1056→        bias: torch.Tensor | None,
  1057→        result_scale: torch.Tensor | None,
  1058→        out_dtype: torch.dtype | None,
  1059→        use_fast_accum: bool,
  1060→    ) -> torch.Tensor:
  1061→        leading_dims = A.shape[:-1]
  1062→        res = torch.ops.aten._scaled_mm(
  1063→            A.flatten(0, -2),
  1064→            B,
  1065→            A_scale,
  1066→            B_scale,
  1067→            bias,
  1068→            result_scale,
  1069→            out_dtype=out_dtype,
  1070→            use_fast_accum=use_fast_accum,
  1071→        )
  1072→        return res.unflatten(0, leading_dims)
  1073→
  1074→    return A.movedim(0, gather_dim), [
  1075→        scaled_matmul(
  1076→            A, B, A_scale, B_scale, bias, result_scale, out_dtype, fast_accum
  1077→        ).movedim(0, gather_dim)
  1078→        for B, B_scale, bias, result_scale, out_dtype, fast_accum in zip(
  1079→            Bs, B_scales, biases, result_scales, out_dtypes, use_fast_accum
  1080→        )
  1081→    ]
  1082→
  1083→
  1084→@torch.library.impl(lib, "fused_all_gather_scaled_matmul", "CUDA")
  1085→def _fused_all_gather_scaled_matmul(
  1086→    A_shard: torch.Tensor,
  1087→    Bs: list[torch.Tensor],
  1088→    A_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1089→    B_scales: list[torch.Tensor],
  1090→    gather_dim: int,
  1091→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1092→    biases: list[torch.Tensor | None],
  1093→    result_scales: list[torch.Tensor | None],
  1094→    out_dtypes: list[torch.dtype | None],
  1095→    use_fast_accum: list[bool],
  1096→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, list[torch.Tensor]]:
  1097→    """
  1098→    Perform the following logic with micro-pipelined computation and
  1099→    communication:
  1100→
  1101→        A = all_gather_tensor(A_shard, gather_dim, group_name)
  1102→        leading_dims = A.shape[:-1]
  1103→        res = torch.ops.aten._scaled_mm(A.flatten(0, -2), B, A_scale, B_scale)
  1104→        res = res.unflatten(0, leading_dims)
  1105→
  1106→    The input `A_scale` can be tensor-wise, row-wise-sharded or
  1107→    row-wise-replicated.
  1108→
  1109→    Optimal stride order for `A_shard` - if `A_shard.movedim(gather_dim, 0)` is
  1110→    contiguous, no extra copy is required for input layout transformation.
  1111→    Otherwise A_shard needs to be copied once.
  1112→    """
  1113→    out_dtypes = _maybe_convert_scalar_types_to_dtypes(out_dtypes)
  1114→
  1115→    if len(biases) != len(Bs):
  1116→        raise ValueError("len(biases) must be the same as len(Bs)")
  1117→    if len(result_scales) != len(Bs):
  1118→        raise ValueError("len(result_scales) must be the same as len(Bs)")
  1119→    if len(out_dtypes) != len(Bs):
  1120→        raise ValueError("len(out_dtypes) must be the same as len(Bs)")
  1121→    if len(use_fast_accum) != len(Bs):
  1122→        raise ValueError("len(use_gast_accum_list) must be the same as len(Bs)")
  1123→
  1124→    if _is_test_mode:
  1125→        return _fused_all_gather_scaled_matmul_fallback(
  1126→            A_shard,
  1127→            Bs,
  1128→            A_scale,
  1129→            B_scales,
  1130→            gather_dim,
  1131→            group_name,
  1132→            biases,
  1133→            result_scales,
  1134→            out_dtypes,
  1135→            use_fast_accum,
  1136→        )
  1137→
  1138→    with torch.profiler.record_function("fused_all_gather_scaled_matmul"):
  1139→        A, res = _fused_all_gather_matmul_impl(
  1140→            torch.ops.aten._scaled_mm.out,
  1141→            A_shard,
  1142→            Bs,
  1143→            A_scale,
  1144→            [
  1145→                {
  1146→                    "scale_b": B_scale,
  1147→                    "bias": bias,
  1148→                    "scale_result": result_scale,
  1149→                    "out_dtype": out_dtype,
  1150→                    "use_fast_accum": fast_accum,
  1151→                }
  1152→                for B_scale, bias, result_scale, out_dtype, fast_accum in zip(
  1153→                    B_scales, biases, result_scales, out_dtypes, use_fast_accum
  1154→                )
  1155→            ],
  1156→            out_dtypes,
  1157→            gather_dim,
  1158→            group_name,
  1159→            True,
  1160→        )
  1161→        assert A is not None
  1162→        return A, res
  1163→
  1164→
  1165→def make_contiguous_for_perm(
  1166→    t: torch.Tensor,
  1167→    perm: list[int],
  1168→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1169→    """
  1170→    Restride `t` such that `t.permute(perm)` is contiguous.
  1171→    """
  1172→    inv_perm = [0] * len(perm)
  1173→    for i, p in enumerate(perm):
  1174→        inv_perm[p] = i
  1175→    return t.permute(perm).contiguous().permute(inv_perm)
  1176→
  1177→
  1178→def restride_A_shard_for_fused_all_gather_matmul(
  1179→    t: torch.Tensor,
  1180→    gather_dim: int,
  1181→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1182→    """
  1183→    Restride the `A_shard` arg of `fused_all_gather_matmul` for optimal perf.
  1184→    See the doc for `fused_all_gather_matmul` for detail.
  1185→    """
  1186→    perm = list(range(len(t.shape)))
  1187→    perm.insert(0, perm.pop(gather_dim))
  1188→    return make_contiguous_for_perm(t, perm)
  1189→
  1190→
  1191→@torch.library.impl(lib, "fused_matmul_reduce_scatter", "CUDA")
  1192→def _fused_matmul_reduce_scatter(
  1193→    A: torch.Tensor,
  1194→    B: torch.Tensor,
  1195→    reduce_op: str,
  1196→    scatter_dim: int,
  1197→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1198→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1199→    """
  1200→    Perform the following logic with micro-pipelined computation and
  1201→    communication:
  1202→
  1203→        reduce_scatter_tensor(A @ B, reduce_op, scatter_dim, group_name)
  1204→
  1205→    Optimal stride order for A - if A.movedim(scatter_dim, 0) is contiguous, no
  1206→    extra copy is required for input layout transformation. Otherwise A needs
  1207→    to be copied once.
  1208→    """
  1209→    if _is_test_mode:
  1210→        return _fused_matmul_reduce_scatter_fallback(
  1211→            A, B, reduce_op, scatter_dim, group_name
  1212→        )
  1213→
  1214→    with torch.profiler.record_function("fused_matmul_reduce_scatter"):
  1215→        return _fused_matmul_reduce_scatter_impl(
  1216→            mm_out_op=torch.ops.aten.mm.out,
  1217→            A=A,
  1218→            B=B,
  1219→            kwargs={},
  1220→            out_dtype=A.dtype,
  1221→            reduce_op=reduce_op,
  1222→            scatter_dim=scatter_dim,
  1223→            group_name=group_name,
  1224→        )
  1225→
  1226→
  1227→@torch.library.impl(lib, "fused_matmul_reduce_scatter", "Meta")
  1228→def _fused_matmul_reduce_scatter_fallback(
  1229→    A: torch.Tensor,
  1230→    B: torch.Tensor,
  1231→    reduce_op: str,
  1232→    scatter_dim: int,
  1233→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1234→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1235→    res = funcol.reduce_scatter_tensor(A @ B, reduce_op, scatter_dim, group_name)
  1236→    res = funcol.wait_tensor(res)
  1237→    return res
  1238→
  1239→
  1240→def _fused_matmul_reduce_scatter_impl(
  1241→    mm_out_op: torch._ops.OpOverload,
  1242→    A: torch.Tensor,
  1243→    B: torch.Tensor,
  1244→    kwargs: dict[str, Any],
  1245→    out_dtype: torch.dtype | None,
  1246→    reduce_op: str,
  1247→    scatter_dim: int,
  1248→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1249→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1250→    if A.dim() < 2:
  1251→        raise ValueError("A_shard must be a matrix")
  1252→    if scatter_dim < 0 or scatter_dim >= A.dim():
  1253→        raise ValueError("Invalid gather_dim")
  1254→    if B.dim() != 2:
  1255→        raise ValueError("B must be a matrix")
  1256→    if reduce_op == "sum":
  1257→        reduce_fn = partial(torch.sum, dim=0)
  1258→    elif reduce_op == "avg":
  1259→        reduce_fn = partial(torch.mean, dim=0)
  1260→    else:
  1261→        raise ValueError("reduce_op must be sum or avg")
  1262→    group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
  1263→    out_shape = [*A.shape[:-1], B.shape[1]]
  1264→    out_shape[scatter_dim] //= group.size()
  1265→
  1266→    if scatter_dim == A.ndim - 1:
  1267→        B_shards = B.chunk(group.size(), dim=B.ndim - 1)
  1268→        A_flat = A.flatten(0, -2)
  1269→
  1270→        def _chunk_producer(rank: int, out: torch.Tensor) -> None:
  1271→            mm_out_op(A_flat, B_shards[rank], **kwargs, out=out)
  1272→
  1273→        leading_dims = list(A.shape[:-1])
  1274→
  1275→        stacked_partials = torch.empty(
  1276→            (A_flat.shape[0], B.shape[1]),
  1277→            dtype=out_dtype or A.dtype,
  1278→            device=A.device,
  1279→        )
  1280→
  1281→        _pipelined_produce_and_all2all(
  1282→            _chunk_producer,
  1283→            stacked_partials,
  1284→            group_name,
  1285→            out_chunk_dim=1,
  1286→        )
  1287→
  1288→        stacked_partials_view = stacked_partials.reshape(
  1289→            *leading_dims, group.size(), -1
  1290→        )
  1291→        return reduce_fn(
  1292→            stacked_partials_view,
  1293→            dim=-2,
  1294→        )
  1295→
  1296→    # Move the scatter_dim to the front and flatten the tensor into a 2D matrix
  1297→    x = A.movedim(scatter_dim, 0)
  1298→    leading_dims = [group.size()] + list(x.shape[:-1])
  1299→    leading_dims[1] //= group.size()
  1300→    x = x.flatten(0, -2)
  1301→    A_shards = x.chunk(group.size())
  1302→
  1303→    # Computing block-wise matmul along the first dim of A
  1304→    def chunk_producer(rank: int, out: torch.Tensor) -> None:
  1305→        mm_out_op(A_shards[rank], B, **kwargs, out=out)
  1306→
  1307→    stacked_partials = x.new_empty(x.shape[0], B.shape[1], dtype=out_dtype or A.dtype)
  1308→
  1309→    _pipelined_produce_and_all2all(
  1310→        chunk_producer,
  1311→        stacked_partials,
  1312→        group_name,
  1313→    )
  1314→
  1315→    # Ensures that the transpose and reduction produce contiguous result
  1316→    # in a single reduction kernel.
  1317→    return reduce_fn(
  1318→        stacked_partials.view(*leading_dims, -1)
  1319→        .movedim(1, scatter_dim + 1)
  1320→        .movedim(0, scatter_dim),
  1321→        dim=scatter_dim,
  1322→    )
  1323→
  1324→
  1325→@torch.library.impl(lib, "fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter", "CUDA")
  1326→def _fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter(
  1327→    A: torch.Tensor,
  1328→    B: torch.Tensor,
  1329→    A_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1330→    B_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1331→    reduce_op: str,
  1332→    orig_scatter_dim: int,
  1333→    scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape: int,
  1334→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1335→    output_shape: list[int],
  1336→    bias: torch.Tensor | None = None,
  1337→    result_scale: torch.Tensor | None = None,
  1338→    out_dtype: torch.dtype | None = None,
  1339→    use_fast_accum: bool = False,
  1340→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1341→    if _is_test_mode:
  1342→        return _fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter_fallback(
  1343→            A,
  1344→            B,
  1345→            A_scale,
  1346→            B_scale,
  1347→            reduce_op,
  1348→            orig_scatter_dim,
  1349→            scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape,
  1350→            group_name,
  1351→            output_shape,
  1352→            bias,
  1353→            result_scale,
  1354→            out_dtype,
  1355→            use_fast_accum,
  1356→        )
  1357→    with torch.profiler.record_function("fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter"):
  1358→        return _fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter_impl(
  1359→            mm_out_op=torch.ops.aten._scaled_mm.out,
  1360→            A=A,
  1361→            B=B,
  1362→            A_scale=A_scale,
  1363→            kwargs={
  1364→                "scale_b": B_scale,
  1365→                "bias": bias,
  1366→                "scale_result": result_scale,
  1367→                "out_dtype": out_dtype,
  1368→                "use_fast_accum": use_fast_accum,
  1369→            },
  1370→            out_dtype=out_dtype,
  1371→            reduce_op=reduce_op,
  1372→            orig_scatter_dim=orig_scatter_dim,
  1373→            scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape=scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape,
  1374→            group_name=group_name,
  1375→            output_shape=output_shape,
  1376→        )
  1377→
  1378→
  1379→@torch.library.impl(lib, "fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter", "Meta")
  1380→def _fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter_fallback(
  1381→    A: torch.Tensor,
  1382→    B: torch.Tensor,
  1383→    A_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1384→    B_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1385→    reduce_op: str,
  1386→    orig_scatter_dim: int,
  1387→    scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape: int,
  1388→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1389→    output_shape: list[int],
  1390→    bias: torch.Tensor | None = None,
  1391→    result_scale: torch.Tensor | None = None,
  1392→    out_dtype: torch.dtype | None = None,
  1393→    use_fast_accum: bool = False,
  1394→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1395→    if A_scale.numel() > 1:
  1396→        if A_scale.shape[:-1] != A.shape[:-1]:
  1397→            raise ValueError(
  1398→                "For row-wise scaling, the leading dims of A_scale "
  1399→                "must match the leading dims of A "
  1400→                f"(A shape: {A.shape}, A_scale shape: {A_scale.shape})"
  1401→            )
  1402→        A_scale = A_scale.flatten(0, -2).contiguous()
  1403→    elif A_scale.numel() != 1:
  1404→        raise ValueError(
  1405→            "Invalid A_scale shape "
  1406→            f"(A shape: {A.shape}, A_scale shape: {A_scale.shape})"
  1407→        )
  1408→
  1409→    C = torch._scaled_mm(
  1410→        A.flatten(0, -2).contiguous(),
  1411→        B,
  1412→        A_scale,
  1413→        B_scale,
  1414→        bias,
  1415→        result_scale,
  1416→        out_dtype,
  1417→        use_fast_accum,
  1418→    )
  1419→    C = C.view(*output_shape[:-1], B.shape[1])
  1420→    res = funcol.reduce_scatter_tensor(
  1421→        C,
  1422→        reduce_op,
  1423→        orig_scatter_dim,  # need original scatter dim for 3D+ output tensor here
  1424→        group_name,
  1425→    )
  1426→    res = funcol.wait_tensor(res)
  1427→    return res
  1428→
  1429→
  1430→def _fused_scaled_matmul_reduce_scatter_impl(
  1431→    mm_out_op: torch._ops.OpOverload,
  1432→    A: torch.Tensor,
  1433→    B: torch.Tensor,
  1434→    A_scale: torch.Tensor,
  1435→    kwargs: dict[str, Any],
  1436→    out_dtype: torch.dtype | None,
  1437→    reduce_op: str,
  1438→    orig_scatter_dim: int,
  1439→    scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape: int,
  1440→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1441→    output_shape: list[int],
  1442→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1443→    if A.dim() < 2:
  1444→        raise ValueError("A_shard must be a matrix")
  1445→    if (
  1446→        scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape < 0
  1447→        or scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape >= A.dim()
  1448→    ):
  1449→        raise ValueError("Invalid scatter dim for 2D tensor input to scaled_mm")
  1450→    if orig_scatter_dim < 0 or orig_scatter_dim >= len(output_shape):
  1451→        raise ValueError("Invalid scatter dim for 3D+ output tensor")
  1452→    if B.dim() != 2:
  1453→        raise ValueError("B must be a matrix")
  1454→    if reduce_op == "sum":
  1455→        reduce_fn = partial(torch.sum, dim=0)
  1456→    elif reduce_op == "avg":
  1457→        reduce_fn = partial(torch.mean, dim=0)
  1458→    else:
  1459→        raise ValueError("reduce_op must be sum or avg")
  1460→
  1461→    group = c10d._resolve_process_group(group_name)
  1462→
  1463→    # Move scatter to first dim, then shard the tensor along the first dim, so the chunk producer
  1464→    # can perform matmuls along the first dim.
  1465→    A_with_scatter_dim_0 = A.movedim(scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape, 0)
  1466→
  1467→    # To handle case where A is 3D+, reshape to 2D to prepare for mm which requires 2D inputs.
  1468→    A_2D_with_scatter_dim_0 = A_with_scatter_dim_0.flatten(0, -2)
  1469→
  1470→    # Partition A along the first dim to prepare for sharding across TP process group.
  1471→    A_shards = A_2D_with_scatter_dim_0.chunk(group.size())
  1472→
  1473→    # Now that 'A' is sharded along the first dim, we need to update its scale(s) accordingly.
  1474→    # How we do this depends on if we are using tensorwise scaling, rowwise scaling, or no scaling.
  1475→    tensorwise_scaling = A_scale is not None and A_scale.numel() == 1
  1476→    rowwise_scaling = A_scale is not None and A_scale.numel() > 1
  1477→
  1478→    # For tensorwise scaling, the scale should be replicated so each shard has a copy.
  1479→    if tensorwise_scaling:
  1480→        A_scale_shards = [A_scale] * group.size()
  1481→
  1482→    # For rowwise scaling, we need to move the scatter dim to the first dim to match the
  1483→    # dim swap of the 'A' tensor. Then we can shard the scales along the first dim, just like
  1484→    # the 'A' tensor.
  1485→    elif rowwise_scaling:
  1486→        if A_scale.shape[:-1] != A.shape[:-1]:
  1487→            raise ValueError(
  1488→                "For row-wise scaling, the leading dims of A_scale "
  1489→                "must match the leading dims of A "
  1490→                f"(A shape: {A.shape}, A_scale shape: {A_scale.shape})"
  1491→            )
  1492→        A_scale = (
  1493→            A_scale.movedim(scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape, 0)
  1494→            .contiguous()
  1495→            .flatten(0, -2)
  1496→        )
  1497→        A_scale_shards = list(A_scale.chunk(group.size()))
  1498→        # cuBLAS's row-wise kernel requires scales to be aligned to 16 bytes.
  1499→        # When we slice them we might break this and need to reallocate them.
  1500→        A_scale_shards = [
  1501→            t if t.data_ptr() % 16 == 0 else t.clone() for t in A_scale_shards
  1502→        ]
  1503→    else:
  1504→        raise ValueError("A_scale cannot be none for scaled_mm")
  1505→
  1506→    # Computing block-wise matmul along the first dim of A
  1507→    def chunk_producer(rank: int, out: torch.Tensor) -> None:
  1508→        mm_out_op(A_shards[rank], B, scale_a=A_scale_shards[rank], **kwargs, out=out)
  1509→
  1510→    # Stacked partials will be the 2D outputs of the pipelined scaled mm, and will
  1511→    # have the shape (A_with_scatter_dim_0_tensor.shape[0], B.shape[1]) to align with the formula:
  1512→    # (a*b,c) @ (c,d) = (a*b,d)
  1513→    stacked_partials = A_with_scatter_dim_0.new_empty(
  1514→        A_2D_with_scatter_dim_0.shape[0], B.shape[1], dtype=out_dtype or A.dtype
  1515→    )
  1516→
  1517→    # Execute the pipelined mm/scaled_mm.
  1518→    _pipelined_produce_and_all2all(
  1519→        chunk_producer,
  1520→        stacked_partials,
  1521→        group_name,
  1522→    )
  1523→
  1524→    # We now need to transform the *unreduced* stacked 2D partial mm outputs to an *unreduced* 3D+ output,
  1525→    # then reduce-scatter. To do this, we first need to determine the shape of the unreduced 3D+ output,
  1526→    # to reshape our stacked partials so we can apply the reduce-scatter.
  1527→    #
  1528→    # The *unreduced* 3D+ tensor will have dim 0 = `group_size`, as we have `group_size` instances of
  1529→    # stacked partial outputs. The next dims will be A's leading dims (sharded along the original scatter dim),
  1530→    # as it was the left operand of the mm op. We can use -1 as the final dim of the view to populate the rest.
  1531→    stacked_partials_3D_leading_dims = [group.size()] + list(
  1532→        # We use A from after the dim swap 0<=>scatter_dim, but before the flatten,
  1533→        # to get the leading dims of the 3D+ view of stacked partials.
  1534→        A_with_scatter_dim_0.shape[:-1]
  1535→    )
  1536→
  1537→    # The `group_size` leading dim has been prepended to `stacked_partials_3D_leading_dims`,
  1538→    # to capture the partial output from each rank. We need to divide the sharding/scatter dim
  1539→    # by the group size. If the original scatter dim was 0, then it is now dim 1 in this
  1540→    # tensor, since this new `group_size` dim was prepended.
  1541→    stacked_partial_scatter_dim = orig_scatter_dim if orig_scatter_dim > 0 else 1
  1542→    stacked_partials_3D_leading_dims[stacked_partial_scatter_dim] //= group.size()
  1543→
  1544→    # Ensures that the transpose and reduction produce contiguous result
  1545→    # in a single reduction kernel.
  1546→    reduced_out = reduce_fn(
  1547→        # View 2D stacked partials as 3D+ tensor of shape (`group_size`, ...)
  1548→        stacked_partials.view(*stacked_partials_3D_leading_dims, -1)
  1549→        # We originally swapped 0<=>scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape. Now after
  1550→        # prepending the `group_size` dim, to undo this original swap, we
  1551→        # must swap 1<=>scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape+1.
  1552→        .movedim(1, scatter_dim_after_maybe_reshape + 1),
  1553→        # Reduce along the `group_size` dim (0).
  1554→        dim=0,
  1555→    )
  1556→
  1557→    # Output shape must be scattered along original scatter dim as well.
  1558→    output_shape[orig_scatter_dim] //= group.size()
  1559→    out = reduced_out.view(*output_shape)
  1560→    return out
  1561→
  1562→
  1563→def restride_A_for_fused_matmul_reduce_scatter(
  1564→    t: torch.Tensor,
  1565→    scatter_dim: int,
  1566→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1567→    """
  1568→    Restride the `A_shard` arg of `fused_matmul_reduce_scatter` for optimal
  1569→    perf. See the doc for `fused_matmul_reduce_scatter` for detail.
  1570→    """
  1571→    perm = list(range(len(t.shape)))
  1572→    perm.insert(0, perm.pop(scatter_dim))
  1573→    return make_contiguous_for_perm(t, perm)
  1574→
  1575→
  1576→def _maybe_convert_scalar_types_to_dtypes(
  1577→    scalar_types: list[Any],
  1578→) -> list[torch.dtype | None]:
  1579→    """
  1580→    When a list of `torch.dtype`s is passed through the dispatcher as
  1581→    `ScalarType[]`, it is converted to a list of scalar type enum values. This
  1582→    function converts it back to a list of `torch.dtype`s.
  1583→    """
  1584→    # Order defined in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/344defc9733a45fee8d0c4d3f5530f631e823196/c10/core/ScalarType.h
  1585→    _SCALAR_TYPE_TO_DTYPE = {
  1586→        0: torch.uint8,
  1587→        1: torch.int8,
  1588→        2: torch.short,
  1589→        3: torch.int,
  1590→        4: torch.int64,
  1591→        5: torch.half,
  1592→        6: torch.float,
  1593→        7: torch.double,
  1594→        8: torch.complex32,
  1595→        9: torch.complex64,
  1596→        10: torch.complex128,
  1597→        11: torch.bool,
  1598→        12: torch.qint8,
  1599→        13: torch.quint8,
  1600→        14: torch.qint32,
  1601→        15: torch.bfloat16,
  1602→        16: torch.float8_e5m2,
  1603→        17: torch.float8_e4m3fn,
  1604→        18: torch.float8_e5m2fnuz,
  1605→        19: torch.float8_e4m3fnuz,
  1606→    }
  1607→    if any(not isinstance(x, (type(None), int)) for x in scalar_types):
  1608→        return scalar_types
  1609→
  1610→    dtypes: list[torch.dtype | None] = []
  1611→    for scalar_type in scalar_types:
  1612→        if scalar_type is None:
  1613→            dtypes.append(scalar_type)
  1614→        elif scalar_type not in _SCALAR_TYPE_TO_DTYPE:
  1615→            raise ValueError(f"Unrecognized scalar type {scalar_type}")
  1616→        else:
  1617→            dtypes.append(_SCALAR_TYPE_TO_DTYPE[scalar_type])
  1618→    return dtypes
  1619→
  1620→
  1621→class Work(_Work):
  1622→    def __init__(self) -> None:
  1623→        super().__init__()
  1624→        self.event = torch.cuda.Event()
  1625→        self.event.record()
  1626→
  1627→    def wait(self, timeout: timedelta = timedelta(seconds=0)) -> bool:
  1628→        self.event.wait()
  1629→        return True
  1630→
  1631→
  1632→"""
  1633→NOTE [low-contention collectives]
  1634→When a collective is overlapped with abundant compute, it makes sense to
  1635→prioritize reducing the contention between the collective and the overlapped
  1636→compute, even at the cost of a slightly slower collective.
  1637→
  1638→Common collective implementations (e.g., NCCL without user buffer
  1639→registration) optimize for throughput with no ambient compute. However, such
  1640→implementations may not be optimal when they are overlapped with compute:
  1641→- These implementations typically fuse the entire collective into a single
  1642→kernel and reserve SM resources based on the most demanding portion of the
  1643→collective, even when a large portion of the collective does not require this
  1644→much resource.
  1645→- These implementations often use SM-based P2P copy as opposed to copy
  1646→engine-based P2P copy. Copy engine-based P2P copy may not have a significant
  1647→advantage when there's no ambient compute. However, it may significantly
  1648→improve overall resource utilization in the presence of ambient compute.
  1649→
  1650→When overlapped with intensive compute (e.g., persistent matmul kernels), the
  1651→SM-usage of a collective can lead to inefficient overlapping.
  1652→
  1653→Low-contention collectives achieve their goals with the following strategies:
  1654→- Use copy engine-based copy whenever possible.
  1655→- Break down portions of a collective with different resource requirements
  1656→into multiple kernels. This improves the overlapping efficiency at the cost
  1657→of additional launching overhead.
  1658→"""
  1659→
  1660→
  1661→@torch.library.impl(lib, "_low_contention_all_gather", "Meta")
  1662→def _low_contention_all_gather_meta(
  1663→    tensor: torch.Tensor,
  1664→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1665→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1666→    group_size = c10d._get_group_size_by_name(group_name)
  1667→    return tensor.new_empty(tensor.shape[0] * group_size, *tensor.shape[1:])
  1668→
  1669→
  1670→@torch.library.impl(lib, "_low_contention_all_gather", "CUDA")
  1671→def _low_contention_all_gather(
  1672→    tensor: torch.Tensor,
  1673→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1674→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1675→    """
  1676→    Performs all-gather with symmetric memory in a low-contention fashion.
  1677→
  1678→    When `tensor` is already in symmetric memory:
  1679→        - The collective is carried out without using SMs.
  1680→        - No symmetric memory workspace is required.
  1681→
  1682→    When `tensor` is not in symmetric memory:
  1683→        - An extra SM-based copy is performed to copy the input data into the
  1684→          symmetric memory workspace.
  1685→        - Symmetric memory workspace size requirement: the size of `tensor`.
  1686→    """
  1687→    symm_mem = rendezvous(tensor, group_name)
  1688→    if symm_mem is not None:
  1689→        input_is_symm_mem = True
  1690→    else:
  1691→        symm_mem = get_symm_mem_workspace(
  1692→            group_name, tensor.numel() * tensor.element_size()
  1693→        )
  1694→        input_is_symm_mem = False
  1695→
  1696→    rank = symm_mem.rank
  1697→    world_size = symm_mem.world_size
  1698→
  1699→    output = tensor.new_empty(tensor.shape[0] * world_size, *tensor.shape[1:])
  1700→    chunks = output.chunk(world_size)
  1701→
  1702→    _get_backend_stream().wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
  1703→    with _get_backend_stream():
  1704→        if not input_is_symm_mem:
  1705→            local_buf = symm_mem.get_buffer(rank, tensor.shape, tensor.dtype)
  1706→            local_buf.copy_(tensor)
  1707→        # pull
  1708→        symm_mem.barrier()
  1709→        for step in range(world_size):
  1710→            remote_rank = (rank - step) % world_size
  1711→            src_buf = symm_mem.get_buffer(remote_rank, tensor.shape, tensor.dtype)
  1712→            chunks[remote_rank].copy_(src_buf)
  1713→        symm_mem.barrier()
  1714→        torch._C._distributed_c10d._register_work(output, Work())
  1715→        return output
  1716→
  1717→
  1718→@torch.library.impl(lib, "_low_contention_reduce_scatter", "Meta")
  1719→def _low_contention_reduce_scatter_meta(
  1720→    tensor: torch.Tensor,
  1721→    reduce_op: str,
  1722→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1723→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1724→    group_size = c10d._get_group_size_by_name(group_name)
  1725→    return tensor.unflatten(0, (group_size, -1)).mean(dim=0)
  1726→
  1727→
  1728→def _low_contention_reduce_scatter_with_symm_mem_input(
  1729→    tensor: torch.Tensor,
  1730→    reduce_op: str,
  1731→    symm_mem: _SymmetricMemory,
  1732→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1733→    rank = symm_mem.rank
  1734→    world_size = symm_mem.world_size
  1735→
  1736→    assert tensor.shape[0] % world_size == 0
  1737→    a2a_res = torch.empty_like(tensor)
  1738→    chunks = a2a_res.chunk(world_size)
  1739→
  1740→    _get_backend_stream().wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
  1741→    with _get_backend_stream():
  1742→        # pull + offline reduction
  1743→        symm_mem.barrier()
  1744→        for step in range(world_size):
  1745→            remote_rank = (rank - step) % world_size
  1746→            src_buf = symm_mem.get_buffer(
  1747→                remote_rank,
  1748→                chunks[0].shape,
  1749→                chunks[0].dtype,
  1750→                chunks[0].numel() * rank,
  1751→            )
  1752→            chunks[remote_rank].copy_(src_buf)
  1753→        symm_mem.barrier()
  1754→
  1755→        ret = a2a_res.unflatten(0, (world_size, -1))
  1756→        if reduce_op == "sum":
  1757→            ret = ret.sum(dim=0)
  1758→        elif reduce_op == "avg":
  1759→            ret = ret.mean(dim=0)
  1760→        else:
  1761→            raise ValueError(f"reduce_op ({reduce_op}) is not supported")
  1762→        torch._C._distributed_c10d._register_work(ret, Work())
  1763→        return ret
  1764→
  1765→
  1766→def _low_contention_reduce_scatter_with_workspace(
  1767→    tensor: torch.Tensor,
  1768→    reduce_op: str,
  1769→    workspace: _SymmetricMemory,
  1770→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1771→    rank = workspace.rank
  1772→    world_size = workspace.world_size
  1773→
  1774→    assert tensor.shape[0] % world_size == 0
  1775→    chunks = tensor.chunk(world_size)
  1776→
  1777→    _get_backend_stream().wait_stream(torch.cuda.current_stream())
  1778→    with _get_backend_stream():
  1779→        # push + offline reduction
  1780→        workspace.barrier()
  1781→        for step in range(world_size):
  1782→            remote_rank = (rank - step) % world_size
  1783→            dst_buf = workspace.get_buffer(
  1784→                remote_rank, chunks[0].shape, chunks[0].dtype, chunks[0].numel() * rank
  1785→            )
  1786→            dst_buf.copy_(chunks[remote_rank])
  1787→        workspace.barrier()
  1788→
  1789→        buf = workspace.get_buffer(rank, tensor.shape, tensor.dtype)
  1790→        ret = buf.unflatten(0, (world_size, -1))
  1791→        if reduce_op == "sum":
  1792→            ret = ret.sum(dim=0)
  1793→        elif reduce_op == "avg":
  1794→            ret = ret.mean(dim=0)
  1795→        else:
  1796→            raise ValueError(f"reduce_op ({reduce_op}) is not supported")
  1797→        torch._C._distributed_c10d._register_work(ret, Work())
  1798→        return ret
  1799→
  1800→
  1801→@torch.library.impl(lib, "_low_contention_reduce_scatter", "CUDA")
  1802→def _low_contention_reduce_scatter(
  1803→    tensor: torch.Tensor,
  1804→    reduce_op: str,
  1805→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1806→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1807→    """
  1808→    Performs reduce-scatter with symmetric memory in a low-contention fashion.
  1809→
  1810→    This implementation performs a P2P-based all-to-all followed by an offline
  1811→    reduction.
  1812→
  1813→    When `tensor` is already in symmetric memory:
  1814→        - Pull-based all-to-all is used.
  1815→        - No symmetric memory workspace is required.
  1816→
  1817→    When `tensor` is not in symmetric memory:
  1818→        - Push-based all-to-all is used.
  1819→        - Symmetric memory workspace size requirement: the size of `tensor`.
  1820→
  1821→    SM-usage:
  1822→        - SM-based copy of the rank's own chunk for the all-to-all.
  1823→        - Reduction on the all-to-all result.
  1824→
  1825→    TODO(yifu): the SM-based copy can be avoided with a list-based reduction
  1826→    kernel.
  1827→    """
  1828→    symm_mem = rendezvous(tensor, group_name)
  1829→    if symm_mem is not None:
  1830→        return _low_contention_reduce_scatter_with_symm_mem_input(
  1831→            tensor, reduce_op, symm_mem
  1832→        )
  1833→    else:
  1834→        workspace = get_symm_mem_workspace(
  1835→            group_name, tensor.numel() * tensor.element_size()
  1836→        )
  1837→        return _low_contention_reduce_scatter_with_workspace(
  1838→            tensor, reduce_op, workspace
  1839→        )
  1840→
  1841→
  1842→@torch.library.impl(lib, "all_to_all_vdev_2d", "Meta")
  1843→def _all_to_all_vdev_2d_meta(
  1844→    input: torch.Tensor,
  1845→    out: torch.Tensor,
  1846→    in_splits: torch.Tensor,
  1847→    out_splits_offsets: torch.Tensor,
  1848→    group_name: c10d.GroupName,
  1849→    major_align: int | None = None,
  1850→) -> None:
  1851→    return None
  1852→
  1853→
  1854→@torch.library.impl(lib, "all_to_all_vdev_2d_offset", "Meta")
  1855→def _all_to_all_vdev_2d_offset_meta(
  1856→    input: torch.Tensor,

  1870→from collections.abc import Sequence
  1871→from typing import overload, TYPE_CHECKING, Union
  1872→
  1873→
  1874→if TYPE_CHECKING:
  1875→    from torch._C._distributed_c10d import ProcessGroup
  1876→    from torch.types import _device, _dtype, _int
  1877→
  1878→
  1879→@overload
  1880→def empty(
  1881→    *size: _int, dtype: _dtype | None = None, device: _device | None = None
  1882→) -> torch.Tensor: ...
  1883→
  1884→
  1885→@overload
  1886→# pyrefly: ignore [inconsistent-overload]
  1887→def empty(
  1888→    size: Sequence[_int],
  1889→    *,
  1890→    dtype: _dtype | None = None,
  1891→    device: _device | None = None,
  1892→) -> torch.Tensor: ...
  1893→
  1894→
  1895→def empty(  # type: ignore[misc]
  1896→    *size: Any,
  1897→    dtype: _dtype | None = None,
  1898→    device: _device | None = None,
  1899→) -> torch.Tensor:
  1900→    r"""
  1901→    Similar to :func:`torch.empty()`. The returned tensor can be used by
  1902→    :func:`torch._distributed._symmetric_memory.rendezvous()` to establish a
  1903→    symmetric memory tensor among participating processes.
  1904→
  1905→    Args:
  1906→        size (int...): a sequence of integers defining the shape of the output tensor.
  1907→            Can be a variable number of arguments or a collection like a list or tuple.
  1908→
  1909→    Keyword args:
  1910→        dtype (:class:`torch.dtype`, optional): the desired data type of returned tensor.
  1911→            Default: if ``None``, uses a global default (see :func:`torch.set_default_dtype`).
  1912→        device (:class:`torch.device`, optional): the desired device of returned tensor.
  1913→            Default: if ``None``, uses the current device for the default tensor type
  1914→            (see :func:`torch.set_default_device`). :attr:`device` will be the CPU
  1915→            for CPU tensor types and the current CUDA device for CUDA tensor types.
  1916→    """
  1917→    if len(size) == 1 and isinstance(size[0], Sequence):
  1918→        size = tuple(size[0])
  1919→    else:
  1920→        size = tuple(size)
  1921→
  1922→    if dtype is None:
  1923→        dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()
  1924→
  1925→    if device is None:
  1926→        device = torch.get_default_device()
  1927→
  1928→    return _SymmetricMemory.empty_strided_p2p(
  1929→        size=size,
  1930→        stride=torch._prims_common.make_contiguous_strides_for(size),
  1931→        dtype=dtype,
  1932→        device=torch.device(device),
  1933→    )

  1935→
  1936→def rendezvous(
  1937→    tensor: torch.Tensor, group: Union[c10d.GroupName, ProcessGroup]
  1938→) -> _SymmetricMemory:
  1939→    r"""
  1940→    rendezvous(tensor, group) -> _SymmetricMemory
  1941→
  1942→    Establish a symmetric memory tensor among participating processes. This is
  1943→    a collective operation.
  1944→
  1945→    Args:
  1946→        tensor (:class:`torch.Tensor`): the local tensor used to establish the symmetric memory tensor.
  1947→            It must be allocated via :func:`torch._distributed._symmetric_memory.empty()`. The shape,
  1948→            dtype, and device type must be identical across all participating processes.
  1949→        group (Union[str, :class:`torch.distributed.ProcessGroup`]): The group identifying the
  1950→            participating processes. This can be either a group name or a process group object.
  1951→    """
  1952→    from torch._C._distributed_c10d import ProcessGroup
  1953→
  1954→    if isinstance(group, str):
  1955→        group_name = c10d.GroupName(group)
  1956→    elif isinstance(group, ProcessGroup):
  1957→        group_name = group.group_name
  1958→    else:
  1959→        raise TypeError(f"rendezvous: unsupported group type: {type(group)}")
  1960→
  1961→    enable_symm_mem_for_group(group_name)
  1962→    return _SymmetricMemory.rendezvous(tensor, group_name)
  1963→
  1964→
  1965→def is_nvshmem_available() -> bool:
  1966→    r"""
  1967→    is_nvshmem_available() -> bool
  1968→
  1969→    Check if NVSHMEM is available in current build and on current system.
  1970→    """
  1971→    try:
  1972→        from torch._C._distributed_c10d import _is_nvshmem_available
  1973→    except ImportError:
  1974→        # Not all builds have NVSHMEM support.
  1975→        return False
  1976→
  1977→    # Check if NVSHMEM is available on current system.
  1978→    return _is_nvshmem_available()
  1979→

  1981→def set_backend(name: Literal["NVSHMEM", "CUDA", "NCCL"]) -> None:
  1982→    r"""
  1983→    Set the backend for symmetric memory allocation. This is a global setting
  1984→    and affects all subsequent calls to
  1985→    :func:`torch._distributed._symmetric_memory.empty()`.  Note that the backend
  1986→    cannot be changed once a symmetric memory tensor has been allocated.
  1987→
  1988→    Args:
  1989→        backend (str): the backend for symmetric memory allocation. Currently,
  1990→            only `"NVSHMEM"`, `"CUDA"`, `"NCCL"` are supported.
  1991→    """
  1992→    _SymmetricMemory.set_backend(name)

  1997→    Get the backend for symmetric memory allocation for a given device. If not
  1998→    found, return None.

  2001→        device (`torch.device` or str): the device for which to get the backend.

  2003→    return _SymmetricMemory.get_backend(torch.device(device))

  2017→def set_signal_pad_size(size: int) -> None:
  2018→    r"""
  2019→    Set the signal pad size for future symmetric memory allocations.
  2020→
  2021→    Signal pads are P2P-accessible memory regions used for synchronization in
  2022→    symmetric memory. This function allows users to configure
  2023→    the signal pad size to be proportional to their workload requirements.
  2024→
  2025→    .. warning::
  2026→        This must be called before any symmetric memory allocations are made.
  2027→        The size cannot be changed after allocations have been performed.
